hero.title: One typed schema. Your whole form stack.
hero.subtitle: Rendered in React, validated with Zod, persisted to Postgres via Prisma or Drizzle. Forms that don't stop at the frontend.

features.title: DAG dependencies
features.body: A value change recomputes only affected fields — O(k), not O(n).

features.title: Conditional logic
features.body: Show, hide, require, or disable fields with compiled AND/OR conditions.

features.title: Multi-step & branching
features.body: Steppers with skip conditions, per-step validation, and branch-to-step logic.

features.title: Framework-agnostic core
features.body: Pure-TypeScript engine. React bindings are stable today; Vue, Svelte, Solid, and Angular adapters are planned.

features.title: Swappable adapters
features.body: Prisma or Drizzle with one interface change. Express, Fastify, Hono, tRPC, GraphQL.

features.title: One typed contract
features.body: Auto-generated Zod keeps client and server validation aligned.

comparison.dfe: DFE adds config-driven defs, workflows, and backend orchestration
DFE adds dependencies, steps, branching, and adapters
DFE is TypeScript-first, package-based, and self-hosted

sponsorTiers.name: Company
sponsorTiers.perk: Docs + README logo · quarterly roadmap input

claudeCode.heading: Build forms with Claude Code
claudeCode.body: Install the plugin and let Claude Code scaffold multi-step flows, starter templates, backend wiring, validation, and migrations. Then just ask: "build a multi-step onboarding form with a Postgres backend."
